title: myrcia jefensis
side_effects: Myrcia jefensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals. In rare cases, excessive consumption of the plant's extracts may lead to allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and difficulty breathing. Prolonged use or high doses of the plant may also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, resulting in adverse effects. Additionally, improper preparation or handling of the plant may lead to contamination, increasing the risk of infections and other health complications.
